steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Extract side equality from the ratio
steps.content:
From $\frac{GH}{GF} = \frac{LM}{LK}$ and the given $GH = LM = 34$ cm, we substitute:
$$\frac{34}{GF} = \frac{34}{LK}$$
Since the numerators are equal, the denominators must be equal:
$$GF = LK$$

steps.title: Verify the three components of SAS
steps.content:
SAS (Side-Angle-Side) requires two sides and the included angle to be equal.

First side: $GF = LK$ ✓
Included angle: $\angle G = \angle L = 30^{\circ}$ (the angle at $G$ lies between sides $GF$ and $GH$; the angle at $L$ lies between sides $LK$ and $LM$) ✓
Second side: $GH = LM = 34$ cm ✓

All three components are satisfied.

steps.title: Apply SAS and conclude
steps.content:
Since both triangles have the same two sides and included angle, triangle $FGH \cong$ triangle $KLM$ by the SAS criterion.

This congruence is established using only the given information, without requiring any additional details about the other angles or sides.

That confirms D is correct.
